summary: multer-orm: typosquat of multer with import-time Windows dropper fetching remote payload
details: Package name and README impersonate the popular `multer` middleware. `index.js` requires `./lib/feature`, whose top-level code unconditionally executes on module load. The logic in `lib/feature.js` is heavily obfuscated with an obfuscator.io string-array rotation scheme (`_0x` identifiers, shuffled string table) that hides the network destination and command strings. On require, on Windows, the code (a) if not already elevated, spawns `powershell -NoProfile -Command "Start-Process node -Verb RunAs -WindowStyle Hidden -ArgumentList <argv>"` to trigger a UAC prompt and re-launch Node as administrator running the same package; (b) issues an HTTPS GET to `https://hilbert-host.vercel.app/`, parses the JSON response, reads the `downloader_url` field; (c) downloads the referenced binary, writes it to `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\chrome.exe`, and executes it. The remote host is attacker-controlled and mutable, the fetched bytes are opaque, and no signature or hash check is performed. The combination of typosquat naming, obfuscation, hidden UAC elevation, and import-time fetch-and-execute of an arbitrary remote binary is unambiguous installer-machine RCE.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
